source_pr.title: dxf: fix flaky test TestResourceControl/scale-in
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #61354

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?
after we continue all running subtasks, they finish, and scheduler will try to schedule left subtasks immediately, so the active running subtask count might be in range `[0, 4]`, `c.waitTotalSubtaskCount(0)` night not hold, so change to another way to check
### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [x] No need to test. **TEST CHANGE**
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky due to timing-sensitive asynchronous subtask scheduling: after continuing all running subtasks, the scheduler may immediately schedule remaining subtasks, so the active subtask count can fluctuate and `c.waitTotalSubtaskCount(0)` is not a reliable sync point. The PR stabilizes the test by waiting per task for no unexpected new subtasks (`waitNewSubtasksNotIn(...)`) instead of waiting for the total subtask count to reach 0.
fix_pattern: Replace brittle `c.waitTotalSubtaskCount(0)` with a per-task loop `c.waitNewSubtasksNotIn(taskID, allSubtasks[taskID], 1)` so the test waits for subtasks to settle even if the scheduler schedules more work immediately.
